revision.text:
[[de:Rainer Hannig]][[en:Rainer Hannig]][[fr:Rainer Hannig]]
{| class="center" style="width:310px;" ! colspan="3" |Drunkenness |- |Name{{Literatur |Titel=Die Sprache der Pharaonen |Autor={{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Hannig|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} |Sammelwerk=Kulturgeschichte der antiken Welt |Band=86 |Verlag=von Zabern |Ort=Mainz |Datum=2000-02-01 |TitelErg=Großes Handwörterbuch Deutsch-Ägyptisch (2800–950 v. Chr.) |Seiten=1280 |DNB=957877323]] | class="hiero" |X1:Aa1-Z4:W3 | rowspan="2" | Techi
''tḫj'' |- |in the ceiling
painting in the tomb
of Senenmut
(schematic,
mirrored) |class="hiero" |
X1:N33-W22
[[File:SenenmutRad.svg|rahmenlos|80px]] |- ! colspan="3" |The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season |class="hiero" |N11:Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" |1st Month
of {{ill|Achet|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}
''1-nw-aḫt'' |- | as before, in the
Ramesseum
(mirrored) | class="hiero" style="color:red;"|N11:N14-N11:|:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |Note:
Wikipedia currently defaults to left-to-right writing for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian hieroglyphs|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}. However, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senenmut|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senenmut|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} († c. 1460 BC), {{ill|Thebes (Egypt)|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Egypt)|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}, {{ill|TT353|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}. A high-resolution scan is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Accessed November 14, 2021) and in the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesseum|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}{{Literatur |Titel=Denkmäler des neuen Reichs |Hrsg=C. R. Lepsius |Sammelwerk=Denkmäler aus Ägypten und Äthiopien nach den Zeichnungen der von seiner Majestät dem Könige von Preußen Friedrich Wilhelm IV nach diesen Ländern gesendeten und in den Jahren 1842-1845 ausgeführten wissenschaftlichen Expedition |Band=Band VI enthaltend III. Abteilung |Verlag=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |Seiten=171 |Ort=Berlin | Datum= 1849|Umfang=Blatt XCI-CLXXII |Online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html]], the direction is reversed.
|}
'''Techi''' was the name for the first month of the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian calendar|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian calendar|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} during the 18th to 20th {{ill|Dynasties of Ancient Egypt|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dynasties of Ancient Egypt|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}. This month followed {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wepet-renpet|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} and is already attested in the tomb chamber of {{ill|Senenmut|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senenmut|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}.{{Literatur |Titel=The Reconstructed Chronology of the Egyptian Kings |Autor=M. Christine Tetley |Hrsg=Barry W. Tetley |Ort=Onerahi (Whangarei, New Zealand) |Datum=2014 |Sprache=en |ISBN=978-0-473-41906-6 |Seiten=86f. |Online=https://archive.org/download/THERECONSTRUCTEDCHRONOLOGYOFTHEEGYPTIANKINGSTHEEBERSCALENDARISPROBABLYTHEMOSTVALUABLE/THE%20RECONSTRUCTED%20CHRONOLOGY%20OF%20-THE%20EGYPTIAN%20KINGS-%20THE%20EBERS%20%20CALENDAR%20%20-IS%20PROBABLY%20THE%20MOST%20VALUABLE.pdf |Format=PDF |Abruf=2021-10-21]] In German-language literature, the name is translated as '''Drunkenness''',S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' In: ''Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Nr. 10. Mainz 1950, p. (40) and Table 10; p. (49). which has been interpreted as a reference to the {{ill|Bastet Festival|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bastet Festival|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}. == Background == In Ancient Egypt, multiple calendars were used simultaneously, making the assignment of a given calendar division to a specific system non-trivial. The {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} was based on the heliacal rising of {{ill|Sirius|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sirius|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}, which was associated with the beginning of the Nile flood. It is believed that the "Festivals of the Time Cycles," whose dates were determined by the new moon, were originally defined within this lunar system. However, these festivals, which were celebrated only once a year, were later tied to the administrative calendar.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' In: ''Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' Nr. 10. Mainz 1950, p. (46). As a result, the Bastet Festival and the {{ill|New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} rarely fell on the first day of the first month of {{ill|Achet|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}. The seasons were the basis for the unambiguous designation of months in the lunar calendar, ensuring that the {{ill|Sothic year|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothic year|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} always began with 1 Achet. Alternatively, months were sometimes named after the most important festival celebrated during that period. In ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt'', it is suggested that the names of the months in the administrative calendar were borrowed from those in the lunar calendar.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Band 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 56 §281. In the lunar calendar, Techi (transliterated as ''tḫy'') was already used for the first month, whereas in the administrative calendar, the name later evolved into {{ill|Djehuti|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Djehuti|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}, which was eventually {{ill|Hellenized|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hellenized|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} as {{ill|Thoth|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thoth|qid=Q218168|1=EN}}.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Band 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45 Table 7. == References == * {{Literatur |Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Richard Anthony Parker|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950]] * {{Literatur |Titel=Altägyptische Festdaten. |Autor={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qid=Q218168|1=EN}} |Hrsg=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur |Sammelwerk=Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse |Nummer=10 |Ort=Mainz |Datum=1950-10-27]] {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Kalendermonate bis zum Anfang des Neuen Reiches]] {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] {{translated page|de|Rainer Hannig|version=242652964|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Rainer Hannig]] [[de:Achet|lt=Achet|1=Season of the Inundation]] [[de:Ägyptische Hieroglyphen]] [[de:Senenmut]] [[de:Theben (Ägypten)]] [[de:TT353]] [[de:Ramesseum]] [[de:Ägyptischer Kalender]] [[de:Dynastien des Alten Ägypten]] [[de:Wepet-renpet]] [[de:Bastet-Fest]] [[de:Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Sirius]] [[de:Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[de:Sothisjahr]] [[de:Djehuti]] [[de:gräzisiert]] [[de:Thot]] [[de:Richard Anthony Parker]] [[de:Siegfried Schott]] [[de:Techi]] [[en:Techi]] [[en:Rainer Hannig]] [[fr:Rainer Hannig]] {{wikidata link|Q2128138}} [[Category:1952 births]] [[Category:2022 deaths]] [[Category:Academic staff of the University of Marburg]] [[Category:Egyptologists]] [[Category:German people]] [[Category:Men]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
